name: qsv-pragmastat
description: Compute pragmatic statistics using the Pragmastat library.

options.description: One-sample confirmatory analysis. Test center/spread against thresholds. Format: metric:value[,metric:value,...]. Mutually exclusive with --twosample and --compare2.
Two-sample confirmatory analysis. Test shift/ratio/disparity against thresholds. Format: metric:value[,metric:value,...]. Mutually exclusive with --twosample and --compare1.
The field delimiter for reading/writing CSV data. Must be a single character. (default: ,)
Check if there is enough memory to load the entire CSV into memory using CONSERVATIVE heuristics. Not valid for stdin.
Probability that bounds fail to contain the true parameter. Lower values produce wider bounds. Must be achievable for the given sample size.
When set, the first row will not be treated as headers.
Write output to <file> instead of stdout.
Select columns for analysis. Uses qsv's column selection syntax. Non-numeric columns appear with n=0. In two-sample mode, all pairs of selected columns are computed.
Compute two-sample estimators for all column pairs.

examples.description: One-sample statistics with very tight bounds (lower misrate)
Compare one-sample center against a threshold
Compare one-sample center and spread against thresholds
Compare two-sample shift and disparity against thresholds
